mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating sharply, because then rate declines are read as genuine easing rather than crisis response.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable to lower.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recessionary threshold signals weakening household income and earnings momentum, which leads equity investors to price weaker profits and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should intensify when PAYEMS is also falling.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with UNRATE at or above 9.5% do not show a negative median 20-day SPX return, or if the signal disappears when PAYEMS is declining month over month.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad domestic demand recovery, supporting sales and cyclical revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if demand improves only because of one series while the other remains weak.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined rise in RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform single-series signals, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in periods when both series are rising together.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after a prior slump signals improving industrial capacity utilization and future earnings leverage, which should lift the S&P 500 over the next week. The short horizon reflects rapid repricing of cyclical growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month over month after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO rebound episodes do not show positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average return is not above the unconditional benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 confirms broad output contraction, which investors interpret as a profit recession and a negative driver of equity prices over the next month. The signal is most reliable when the contraction is not already offset by an aggressive policy easing regime.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly print is below its prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the sign is neutralized when FEDFUNDS is already near zero and stable.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series signals payroll losses and deteriorating labor income, which compresses consumption expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest when UNRATE is simultaneously rising.
failure_condition: Reject if negative PAYEMS changes do not predict lower 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal is absent when UNRATE is rising over the same window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near the zero lower bound, investors infer rising inflation pressure without immediate policy defense, which compresses real returns and leads to negative S&P 500 performance over the next quarter. The effect weakens if nominal rates are already moving up.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation, low-rate episodes do not produce negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and GDPC1 indicates stronger nominal and real demand, which supports revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. This mechanism is falsified if one series improves while the other deteriorates, because the demand confirmation is incomplete.
failure_condition: Reject if combined RSAFS and GDPC1 gains do not outperform either proxy alone, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive when both demand measures improve together.
